setting.note: Lermontov's portrait of the bored, brilliant, destructive Pechorin plays out across the Russian Caucasus of the 1830s. Its heart is the spa society of Pyatigorsk and Kislovodsk, where Pechorin toys with Princess Mary and kills the vain Grushnitsky in a duel on Mount Mashuk, the very mountain where Lermontov himself would die in a duel a year after the book appeared. Two further tales range out to the smugglers' port of Taman on the Black Sea and to a frontier fort on the Georgian Military Road.

rights.originalWork: In copyright as mapped here; the original Russian (Mikhail Lermontov, 1840) is public domain. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of any translation's text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: A Hero of Our Time, Mikhail Lermontov (1840). Locations mapped by Read the Places.

palette.note: The cold blue of the Caucasus heights and of Pechorin's detachment, the mountain distance he keeps from everyone he wounds. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
